title: TermiPass NeedsLogin Blocker Audit And Recovery Path Research
objective: Audit, inspect, and research viable methods to resolve the workstation-side TermiPass or LarePass blocker after Packet 002 closed blocked with `BackendState: NeedsLogin`, zero node key, no auth URL, no mesh IP, and no usable `100.64.*` route, without performing installs, auth changes, or host-runtime mutation.
dependency_note: Packet 002 proved that the previously documented TermiPass named-pipe and Headscale registration recipe no longer produces a pending node key, auth URL, mesh IP, or usable `100.64.*` route from the current workstation. The next truthful move is a research-only audit packet that identifies viable recovery paths and explicit blockers before any further recovery execution packet is authored.
required_inputs: The previously successful 2026-05-01 recovery record in ops/agents/handoffs/2026-05-01-olares-private-stack-browser-terminal-bring-up-handoff.md and docs/architecture/OLARES-SSH-HOSTKEY-RECONCILIATION-2026-05-01.md
Current workstation read-only access to TermiPass named-pipe local API, Windows service state, network adapter state, route table, installed client artifacts, and any readable logs or config surfaces
Official Olares source context from the upstream beclab/Olares repository and LarePass documentation so the research can distinguish the intended private VPN path from public FRP access
The Step 3 and roadmap authority that keep expansion closed until access and runtime evidence are current
constrained_outputs: A dated handoff that classifies the blocking items into confirmed blockers, likely root-cause candidates, and viable bounded recovery methods
A comparison between the 2026-05-01 successful recovery posture and the current blocked posture
A source-context cross-check showing whether official Olares docs reinforce LarePass VPN over Tailscale/Headscale as the controlling private path
A shortlist of viable next recovery methods with prerequisites, required privilege level, expected evidence, and risk

A tightened list of config evidence that the next execution packet must capture, including local LarePass profile and VPN status, local TermiPass status/prefs/profiles, Headscale device list or pending-node visibility, SSH-over-VPN setting status, and private route plus SSH reachability to 100.64.0.1
A recommendation for whether a new execution packet is justified and, if so, what exact bounded method it should test next
No installs, no promotions, no ingress changes, no auth changes, no host-runtime mutation, no AI-toolchain rollout, no hosting changes
validation_steps: Inspect read-only TermiPass named-pipe status, prefs, profiles, and any locally readable state surfaces to determine why status remains NeedsLogin with zero node key.

Inspect Windows service state, startup mode, dependent processes, adapter state, route table, and readable client logs or config artifacts without restarting services unless a later packet explicitly authorizes that.
Compare the current blocked workstation state against the documented 2026-05-01 recovered state and identify what evidence is now missing.

Cross-check official Olares repo and LarePass documentation to confirm whether the intended private path is LarePass VPN over Tailscale/Headscale rather than public FRP SSH, and use that only as source context rather than execution authority.

Determine whether the viable next methods fall into workstation-local auth recovery, elevated Windows service control, alternative local client state reset, browser-mediated auth bootstrap, or host-side registration preconditions.

Name the exact config evidence the next packet must capture: local LarePass profile and VPN status, TermiPass local API status/prefs/profiles, Headscale device list or pending node visibility, SSH-over-VPN setting state, and private route plus SSH reachability to 100.64.0.1.
Record which candidate methods are admissible as bounded next packets and which would violate current no-go items.
Do not install or remove clients, do not change ingress or auth posture, do not mutate host runtime, and do not widen into generic Olares implementation while executing this packet.
sql_permission_note: This packet authorizes read-only workstation blocker audit and recovery-path research only. It does not authorize database writes, runtime mutation, new Olares installs, ingress changes, or hosting changes.
closure_note: Executed on 2026-05-03 and closed as complete research only. The research confirmed that the likely blocker is workstation-local LarePass or TermiPass profile state, not basic reachability to the Headscale key endpoint. An official Olares source cross-check reinforced that the intended private path is LarePass VPN over Tailscale/Headscale rather than public FRP SSH. The next execution packet should therefore test interactive local profile rehydration or a separately authorized local service-control path and must capture local LarePass profile and VPN state, local TermiPass status/prefs/profiles, Headscale device list or pending-node visibility, SSH-over-VPN setting status, and private route plus SSH reachability to 100.64.0.1.
next_action: Treat Packet 003 as complete, stop routing Prompt 5 as the next live task, and author the next bounded execution packet around interactive local LarePass profile rehydration or the browser-terminal host-inventory fallback using the tightened config-evidence checklist from the Packet 003 handoff.
